segments.text: Then the LORD said to Moses,
Speak to the Israelites and say to them, ‘When someone makes a special vow to the LORD involving the value of persons,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: if the valuation concerns a male from twenty to sixty years of age, then your valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver, according to the sanctuary shekel.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Or if it is a female, then your valuation shall be thirty shekels.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And if the person is from five to twenty years of age, then your valuation for the male shall be twenty shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now if the person is from one month to five years of age, then your valuation for the male shall be five shekels of silver, and for the female three shekels of silver.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And if the person is sixty years of age or older, then your valuation shall be fifteen shekels for the male and ten shekels for the female.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if the one making the vow is too poor to pay the valuation, he is to present the person before the priest, who shall set the value according to what the one making the vow can afford.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he vows an animal that may be brought as an offering to the LORD, any such animal given to the LORD shall be holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He must not replace it or exchange it, either good for bad or bad for good. But if he does substitute one animal for another, both that animal and its substitute will be holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if the vow involves any of the unclean animals that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD, the animal must be presented before the priest.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priest shall set its value, whether high or low; as the priest values it, the price will be set.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If, however, the owner decides to redeem the animal, he must add a fifth to its value.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now if a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD, then the priest shall value it either as good or bad. The price will st and just as the priest values it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if he who consecrated his house redeems it, he must add a fifth to the assessed value, and it will belong to him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If a man consecrates to the LORD a parcel of his land, then your valuation shall be proportional to the seed required for it—fifty shekels of silver for every homer of barley seed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee, the price will st and according to your valuation.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee, the priest is to calculate the price in proportion to the years left until the next Year of Jubilee, so that your valuation will be reduced.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And if the one who consecrated the field decides to redeem it, he must add a fifth to the assessed value, and it shall belong to him.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If, however, he does not redeem the field, or if he has sold it to another man, it may no longer be redeemed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When the field is released in the Jubilee, it will become holy, like a field devoted to the LORD; it becomes the property of the priests.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now if a man consecrates to the LORD a field he has purchased, which is not a part of his own property,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: then the priest shall calculate for him the value up to the Year of Jubilee, and the man shall pay the assessed value on that day as a sacred offering to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought—the original owner of the land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Every valuation will be according to the sanctuary shekel, twenty gerahs to the shekel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But no one may consecrate a firstborn of the livestock, because a firstborn belongs to the LORD. Whether it is an ox or a sheep, it is the LORD’s.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if it is among the unclean animals, then he may redeem it according to your valuation and add a fifth of its value. If it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Nothing that a man sets apart to the LORD from all he owns—whether a man, an animal, or his inherited land—can be sold or redeemed; everything so devoted is most holy to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No person set apart for destruction may be ransomed; he must surely be put to death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Thus any tithe from the land, whether from the seed of the land or the fruit of the trees, belongs to the LORD; it is holy to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If a man wishes to redeem part of his tithe, he must add a fifth to its value.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Every tenth animal from the herd or flock that passes under the shepherd’s rod will be holy to the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He must not inspect whether it is good or bad, and he shall not make any substitution. But if he does make a substitution, both the animal and its substitute shall become holy; they cannot be redeemed.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: These are the commandments that the LORD gave to Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
